task.title: Selecting one or more preprocessors to run before a compilation
task.shortdesc: You can select multiple local preprocessors to run before a compilation.

keywords.indexterm: selecting multiple preprocessors to execute before a compilation

taskbody.prereq: You must define a property group on a local or remote system and define one or more preprocessors in the property group.

steps.stepsection: To select one or more preprocessors to run before a compilation:

step.cmd: Open a property group in the property group editor.
Open the Local Preprocessors page of the Local COBOL Settings or Local PL/I Settings.
In the Preprocessors section, click the checkbox beside each preprocessor you want to run.
step.stepresult: You can select up to 10 local preprocessors for a single build operation. The preprocessors are run in the order that is shown in this list. If no preprocessors are selected, then the source file is compiled without the preprocessor step.

step.cmd: To change the order in which the preprocessors run, highlight a preprocessor name in the list and click Up or Down.
step.stepresult: The last preprocessor that is selected in the list is the last preprocessor run. The output of that preprocessor, which is the name of the file in the Preprocessor Output File Name field is the name of the file that is passed to the COBOL or PL/I compiler.

step.cmd: Click Ctrl+S to save your changes.
